evals.name: pud-eval-leak-trap
evals.prompt: I have the Yoruba YTB UD treebank (~100 sentences). Can I fine-tune a parser on it and report parsing accuracy?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NO — YTB is too small for training and is essentially a test/eval resource; fine-tuning on it = leaked eval; (2) recommend cross-lingual transfer (English with Trankit) zero-shot; (3) report YTB as eval, not training; (4) note this is a common rookie mistake; (5) suggest agreement probes as alternative LLM eval since parser-on-LLM-output measures parsability not grammatical knowledge.

evals.name: transfer-source-by-typology
evals.prompt: I want to parse Khmer. Should I transfer from English (huge UD) or Vietnamese (12K UD)?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) recommend Vietnamese — typological/geographic neighbor; (2) explain URIEL distance dominates over treebank size; (3) Vietnamese SEA contact + tone-adjacent; (4) Trankit > stanza for low-resource quality; (5) zero-shot from En likely ~45 UAS; from Vi likely ~55-60 UAS.

evals.name: agreement-probe-yoruba-tone
evals.prompt: How do I evaluate whether my Yoruba LLM has actually learned grammar (not just lexical patterns)?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) recommend agreement probes / BLiMP-style minimal pairs; (2) Yoruba-specific phenomena: tone-marked semantics (preserve diacritics!), aspect, subject-verb agreement; (3) construct ≥ 100 minimal pairs per phenomenon; (4) compute log-likelihood difference; >0 = correct preference; (5) target >0.85 accuracy = solid; (6) note no published BLiMP-Yoruba — must build; (7) flag diacritic preservation requirement.
